title: MirrorTrace Takedown / Removal-Letter Generator Input
description: Drafts removal requests from the SELF subject's OWN audit findings. Drafting is a first-person action ('remove information about ME'), so the scope enum intentionally omits consented/brand/safety_evidence and accepts only self/public_figure. This actor fetches nothing and analyses no third party; it only reformats existing module_events into standard legal request shapes.

scope_type.title: Scope type
scope_type.description: Takedown drafting is permitted ONLY for your own footprint (self) or a genuine public figure acting publicly. Other compliant scopes (consented/brand/safety_evidence) intentionally cannot run this actor.

findings_dataset_name.title: Findings dataset name
findings_dataset_name.description: Name of the Apify dataset holding the typed module_events produced by the other MirrorTrace actors (PII/tracker/secret/breach/metadata findings). Records that are not valid module_events are ignored. Leave empty to pass findings inline via 'events'.

events.title: Inline findings (module_events)
events.description: Optional: pass module_event records directly (for offline/composed runs). Each must be a real module_event; anything else is dropped. No fake data is generated to fill gaps.

owned_hosts.title: Hosts you control
owned_hosts.description: Hostnames the subject controls (e.g. your-name.com). Findings on these become a self-remediation checklist instead of a third-party data-subject request, because you can simply remove them yourself.

subject_name.title: Your name (for the letter)
subject_name.description: Optional. Used to fill the requester name in the drafted letters. Left blank, it becomes an explicit [[ FILL IN ]] placeholder — never fabricated.

subject_label.title: Subject label
subject_label.description: Human label for the subject (e.g. your name). Used only for reporting and routed through the scope gate's free-text laundering scan.
